seoTitle: How to Change Fonts in Webflow Site-Wide
metaDescription: Update your website's typography globally in Webflow by connecting Google Fonts or uploading custom font files.
intro: Refreshing your typography can transform how a site feels, and Webflow makes it possible to update fonts project-wide instead of editing each heading individually. This guide covers connecting a Google Font or uploading custom font files, then applying the change globally through the Body tag. It's for designers establishing a consistent typography system.

steps.heading: Open Site Settings Fonts
steps.body: Go to your project's dashboard, open Site Settings, and navigate to the Fonts tab.

steps.heading: Choose or upload a font
steps.body: Select a typeface from the Google Fonts library, or upload optimized WOFF2 files for a custom font not hosted there.

steps.heading: Select font weights
steps.body: Pick only the specific weights you actually plan to use to keep your stylesheet size manageable.

steps.heading: Apply the font globally
steps.body: Open the Typography style manager and assign the new typeface to the Body (All Pages) tag so it cascades across your entire site.

steps.heading: Fine-tune headings and test responsiveness
steps.body: Adjust font size, weight, and line-height for headings and body text, then check how typography scales across tablet and mobile breakpoints.

faq.question: Do I need to change fonts on every page individually?
faq.answer: No, applying the new typeface to the Body (All Pages) tag updates it site-wide in one step.

faq.question: Should I enable every font weight available?
faq.answer: No, only enable the weights you actually use, since extra weights add unnecessary load to your stylesheet.

faq.question: Can I use a font that isn't on Google Fonts?
faq.answer: Yes, you can upload your own WOFF2 font files or connect an Adobe Fonts project through the API.

faq.question: Will changing fonts affect my page load speed?
faq.answer: It can, especially with multiple weights or custom uploads, so keep your font selection lean for the best performance.
